title: Common and proper nouns
description: Learn to distinguish common and proper nouns from one another!

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the nouns in this sentence are proper nouns?**

>The penguins had always wanted to go to Malaysia.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Proper nouns are the names given to specific people, places, or things.  They're always capitalized.
**"Malaysia"** is the name of a country, so it's a proper noun.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the nouns in this sentence are proper nouns?**

>Billy cautiously put the eggs in his basket.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Proper nouns are the names of specific people, places, and things.  They're always capitalized.
**"Billy"** is the name of a person, so it's a proper noun.
